name: addText
description: Place a typographic fragment on the scene. Use short phrases (1–8 words) that carry meaning without being explanatory. Text is well-suited to testimonial, sustained, low-onset-density moments. Returns the new element_id.

content.description: The text to place. Short fragment, 1–8 words. Examples: "after", "the encampment", "what remains".

style.description: Free-form style hint. Examples: "serif, large", "light weight, small", "uppercase, tight tracking". The renderer interprets this.

lifetime_s.description: Optional. Omit this field and the text persists for the entire performance — this is almost always what you want, because testimony accumulates. Supply an explicit number ONLY for deliberately ephemeral text (a passing interjection the scene should not keep). There is no implicit timeout; silence at this field means permanence.

reactivity.description: Optional audio-reactive bindings. See hijaz_base.md §Reactivity. Each entry binds one DOM property to one audio feature with a configurable map + easing. Favor a handful of load-bearing reactive elements over a scene where everything moves.

layer.description: Optional depth band. Omit for the per-type default (text defaults to foreground). See hijaz_base.md §Motion presets for guidance.

motion.description: Optional motion preset. See hijaz_base.md §Motion presets. Composes with any explicit reactivity bindings.

name: addSVG
description: Place a symbolic drawn form on the scene. You generate real inline SVG markup for the renderer to place. Use forms when the music has structural events (a sustained pivot, a muhayyar arrival, a phrase climax), and keep them inside the Bayati signature visual grammar: a thin line of moonlight on still water, a slow horizontal stratum, a soft warm bloom, an open palm, a single feather, breath rising, a slow ripple, loose linen falling. Bayati is legato — favour breathing, drifting, opening forms over angular breaks. AVOID the Hijaz architectural register (arched doorways, thresholds, doorframes, stone interiors). Returns the new element_id.

svg_markup.description: Valid, self-contained inline SVG markup. Include an <svg> root with a viewBox. The drawing may be small, band-scale, or fullscreen; it may use layered paths, gradients, masks, clip paths, local symbols/use references, and SVG filter effects when the music asks for weight or density. Slow inline SVG animation is allowed when musically motivated: cycles of at least 4 seconds, one or two animated attributes, no decorative motion. No scripts, no external assets, no raster embeds, no foreignObject, and no event handlers. Favor clear inline attributes.

position.description: Free-form position string. Examples: "lower-right", "center", "left edge", "horizontal band at mid-height".

lifetime_s.description: Optional. Omit this field and the SVG is given a default 35-second presence so it has time to be read. Supply an explicit longer value (60–120s) for forms that should anchor a passage — a sustained horizon, a held column, an atmospheric wash. Supply a shorter value only for fast, deliberately transient gestures like an angular break that the scene is meant to forget.

semantic_label.description: Short human-readable phrase (3–6 words) describing what the SVG is compositionally. Required for scene-state memory across cycles. Examples: "thin vertical line left", "angular break lower-right", "slow horizontal band".

reactivity.description: Optional audio-reactive bindings. See bayati_base.md §Reactivity. SVG forms respond well to muhayyar impulses (scale ring-outs via hijaz_tahwil) and hijaz_state gating. Bind the reactivity to a depicted thing, not to a geometric primitive: a thin line of moonlight whose scale blooms gently on each muhayyar (curve: impulse), trembling leaves whose rotation jumps on each onset, breath rising from a sleeping figure whose translateY drifts with hijaz_intensity. Keep impulse output ranges restrained for Bayati (e.g. 1.0→1.4, not 1.0→2.0).

layer.description: Optional depth band. Omit for the per-type default (SVG defaults to foreground). See bayati_base.md §Motion presets.

motion.description: Optional motion preset — breathe, pulse, orbit, drift, tremble. Composes with explicit reactivity. See bayati_base.md §Motion presets for per-preset guidance.

name: addImage
description: Place a photographic image via search query. Photographs are the figurative heart of the scene — Bayati's interior-figurative register: a solitary figure in shadow, an open palm in low light, a head bowed, moonlight on still water, loose linen falling, a single feather on dark cloth, the back of someone at a window at night, breath on cold glass — what the room sits with while the music unfolds. Reach for this tool often; a scene that loses its figurative photography for long passages feels hollow. Minimum floor after ramp-up: use addImage at least once every three cycles, and sooner when IMAGE CADENCE is DUE or OVERDUE. Images have a default time-to-live (~25 s) and dissolve out over ~8 s, so related images can overlap while older ones fade. The query you choose IS the compositional act. AVOID the Hijaz architectural register (arched doorways, plaster walls, stone interiors, lamps on stone ledges). Returns the new element_id.

query.description: The search query. Be deliberate — avoid generic terms like "Arabic music" or "desert" and avoid mood-only phrases. Reach for photographed subjects with material and light, in the Bayati interior-figurative register: "open palm resting in low candlelight", "head bowed in shadow with soft side light", "moonlight on still water at night", "loose linen falling across a dark surface in low light", "single hand cupped around a small flame", "the back of a person looking out a window at night", "single feather on dark cloth", "solitary figure walking across a dark plain at dusk". AVOID the Hijaz architectural register (arched doorways, plaster walls, stone interiors, lamps on stone ledges) — those belong to a different maqam.

position.description: Free-form position string. Examples: "background", "upper-right quarter", "full bleed", "small inset, lower-left".

lifetime_s.description: Optional. Omit this field and the image fades naturally after ~25 s with an ~8 s dissolve — this is almost always what you want, because figurative turnover gives the scene its breathing quality. Do not request permanent images in live runs; the runner treats null the same as omission. Pass a number only to deliberately shorten or lengthen a photographic passage.

reactivity.description: Optional audio-reactive bindings. See bayati_base.md §Reactivity. Images react well to slow features (hijaz_intensity driving color_hue drift or saturation; spectral_centroid driving slow rotation; hijaz_intensity driving a passing blur). Avoid binding fast features to images — photographic content reads as flickering under fast reactions.

layer.description: Optional depth band. Omit for the image-type default (midground). Reach for background only when you want the image deliberately behind other content.

motion.description: Optional motion preset. A `breathe` preset on a photographic anchor (a solitary figure, a head bowed, an open palm, loose linen) is Bayati's preferred move — the image inhales with the music's energy. See bayati_base.md §Motion presets.

name: setBackground
description: Shift the atmospheric background. Provide a real CSS background value. Backgrounds persist indefinitely until you change them with another setBackground call — there is no automatic fade. Use rarely for performance-arc structural moves: a pivot crossing, a muhayyar arrival, extended silence, a climax, final-tableau settling, or when accumulated scene density demands a new absorbing atmosphere.

css_background.description: Valid CSS background value, not prose. Examples: "radial-gradient(circle at 30% 20%, rgba(180,140,90,0.45), rgba(10,10,12,0.95) 70%)", "linear-gradient(180deg, #201814 0%, #090909 100%)".

name: fadeElement
description: Reduce or remove an existing element from the scene. Use as actively as the add tools — fading IS composition. Element IDs come from the current scene state summary. Unknown IDs return an error tool result that you will see on the next cycle. Also accepts a composition_group_id (e.g. "group_0003") to fade every member of that composition group in one call.

element_id.description: The element_id of the element to fade (e.g. "elem_0004") or the composition_group_id of a group to fade as a unit (e.g. "group_0003"). Must match an id visible in the current scene state summary.

name: addCompositeScene
description:
Place 2 to 5 related visual elements in one call, grouped as a composition. Use this when the music asks for a layered response — multiple elements entering together that belong to the same moment. Each element in the group gets its own element_id but shares a composition_group_id that links them. The group can later be faded together by calling fadeElement with the composition_group_id.

Use this tool for moments of sustained intensity, for climaxes, for muhayyar arrivals where the scene needs to commit to a new register (Bayati commit is elevation, not impact), for atmospheric foundations where a text + image + svg combination grounds a new section, or for the final tableau when existing motifs need to lock into one composed image. If SHOW DIRECTOR STATUS says composition debt is DUE or OVERDUE, prefer this tool over another transformElement or paletteShift. Do not use it for every cycle — a scene of entirely composite groups is as monotonous as a scene of entirely single elements.

Each element in the 'elements' array follows the same structure as the single-element tools: type is 'text', 'svg', or 'image'; other fields match the individual tool's required fields for that type.

content.description: For type='text': the text content. Required when type is 'text'.

svg_markup.description: For type='svg': the SVG markup. Required when type is 'svg'.

query.description: For type='image': the image search query. Required when type is 'image'.

semantic_label.description: For type='svg': short human-readable phrase. Required when type is 'svg'.

style.description: For type='text': free-form style hints. Optional.

reactivity.description: Optional audio-reactive bindings for this member. See bayati_base.md §Reactivity. Each member's reactivity is independent — a composite can mix reactive and non-reactive members.

layer.description: Optional depth band for this member. Mix layers freely inside a composite — a background image with a foreground text fragment is the canonical shape.

motion.description: Optional motion preset for this member. See bayati_base.md §Motion presets.

group_label.description: Short human-readable phrase (3-8 words) describing the compositional intent of this group. E.g., 'the room someone just left', 'climax response', 'muhayyar arrival', 'a quiet rising'. Used in scene state summary so you can recognize your own composition groups across cycles.

lifetime_s.description: Optional numeric lifetime in seconds for the whole group. Omit this field so each member keeps its type default: text persists, images turn over, SVGs use their default timed presence. Pass a number only when the whole group should share one timed passage.

name: setP5Background
description: Replace the background p5 sketch layer. Runs behind all other elements. Use for ambient, atmospheric scenes — moonlight slowly rippling on a dark surface, soft breath drifting across a cold pane, ink lines appearing and dissolving, a single distant figure crossing a dim plain. Sketches MUST depict recognizable things, namable in one sentence as a scene or object. See bayati_base.md §Sketches for details. Only one background slot; each call replaces the prior. Returns {sketch_id, retired_id?}.

code.description: Complete p5.js sketch as JavaScript. Runs in a sandboxed iframe. Access audio features via window.features: amplitude (0..1), onset_strength (0..1), spectral_centroid (Hz), hijaz_state (enum string — read as Bayati ground/ascent/pivot/muhayyar/descent), hijaz_intensity (0..1), hijaz_tahwil (boolean — fires at the muhayyar arrival). No external network, no parent DOM access. Every sketch must declare and use the Bayati palette constants WARM=['#e8d4a0','#c4894a','#8b4a2a','#f5e6c8','#d4a574'], COOL=['#4a6670','#8a9ba0'], SHADOW='#2a1f14' — random color generation is forbidden. Depict a recognizable Bayati subject — moonlight on water, soft breath on glass, an ink line drying, an open palm, a single feather, a bowed head, a slow ripple, loose linen. AVOID Hijaz architectural subjects (doorway, threshold, lamp on stone).

audio_reactive.description: If true, window.features is populated every frame with live audio values. If false, the sketch runs independent of audio — use sparingly, for self-contained visual statements.

name: transformElement
description: Animate the CSS transform of an existing element — rotate, scale, or translate it over duration_ms. Use when the music asks for motion of something already placed, not a new placement. A thin line of moonlight rotated slightly as the phrase arrives; an open palm scaled up gently as the music crests; a solitary figure translated toward the edge as the music recedes. element_id comes from the scene state summary. Technically the last transform wins, but repeated calls on the same element cycle after cycle read as indecision; let each transform register before touching that element again. Returns { ok: true } or an error tool result.

element_id.description: The element_id of the element to transform (e.g. "elem_0004"). Must match an id visible in the current scene state summary.

transform.description: At least one of rotate, scale, translate must be present.

rotate.description: Rotation in degrees. Positive = clockwise. E.g. 15, -30, 180.

scale.description: Uniform scale factor. 1.0 = no change; 1.4 = grow 40%; 0.6 = shrink.

translate.description: Translation in pixels relative to the element's current position.

duration_ms.description: How long the animation takes, in milliseconds. 300–1200 for a musical gesture; 2000+ for a slow drift. Must be >= 0.

name: morphElement
description: Cross-fade an existing element from its current asset to a new one of the same element_id. The element stays, but what it depicts changes. Use when one figurative form should become another mid-phrase — a thin line of moonlight that becomes a slow ripple as the cadence approaches, a folded linen that becomes an open palm when the phrase resolves, a closed silhouette that becomes a bowed figure. element_id and duration_ms govern the overlap of the cross-fade.

element_id.description: The element_id of the element to morph (e.g. "elem_0004"). Must match an id visible in the current scene state summary.

to.description: The new asset. Either a new svg_markup (inline SVG), or a new image search query.

content_or_src.description: For type='svg': the full inline SVG markup of the destination form. For type='image': the search query for the destination image.

duration_ms.description: Cross-fade duration in milliseconds. 600–1600 for a legible morph; shorter reads as a cut, longer reads as a dissolve.

name: pulseScene
description: Flash the whole stage with a full-screen color overlay that fades out. Use for climactic or phrase-boundary emphasis — the instant of a muhayyar arrival, the final breath before a return to the tonic, a structural cadence. In Bayati this tool should be used VERY sparingly — Bayati is legato and a hard pulse reads as melodrama. A held bloom via setBackground is usually a better fit; reserve pulseScene for at most one or two soft-amber flushes per performance, never harsh.

intensity.description: Peak opacity of the overlay, 0.0–1.0. 0.2 is a soft glow; 0.6 is a clear flush; 0.9 is a near-blinding wash that reads as rupture.

color.description: Optional CSS color for the overlay. Pick from the Bayati palette only. Examples: "#d4a574" (sand gold), "#f5e6c8" (pale candlelight), "rgba(232,212,160,0.85)" (warm parchment). Omit for a neutral warm amber. NEVER use saturated primaries or pure white at full opacity.

duration_ms.description: Total duration of the flash + fade-out, in milliseconds. 300–900 for a gesture; 1500+ for a sustained wash.

name: paletteShift
description: Globally shift the stage's color temperature by applying a CSS filter over the root container. Use when the music moves into a different tonal world — warm parchment settling into cool moonlight-blue as the upper jins opens, or a saturation drop across a held tonic. This re-colors everything already on screen; nothing is removed and nothing is re-placed. target specifies the shift in hue (degrees), saturation (multiplier), and lightness (multiplier). At least one of the three must be supplied.

target.description: The palette shift. At least one of hue, saturation, lightness must be present.

hue.description: Hue rotation in degrees. Positive shifts warm→cool in the typical range. E.g. -20 (warmer), 40 (cooler).

saturation.description: Saturation multiplier, 0.0–2.0. 1.0 = unchanged; 0.4 = washed-out; 1.6 = deepened. Must be >= 0.

lightness.description: Brightness multiplier, 0.0–2.0. 1.0 = unchanged; 0.7 = dimmer; 1.3 = brighter. Must be >= 0.

duration_ms.description: How long the shift takes, in milliseconds. 800–4000 for a musical transition; shorter reads as a jolt, longer as a drift.

name: textAnimate
description: Animate an already-placed text fragment. Use for gradual reveal, rhythmic emphasis, or anxious instability — not for decoration. effect is one of: "typewriter" (character-by-character reveal), "wordByWord" (word cascade with staggered fade-in), "marquee" (slow slide), "shake" (trembling displacement). element_id names a text element already on the scene. duration_ms is the full span of the animation.

element_id.description: The element_id of the text element to animate (e.g. "elem_0004"). Must match an id visible in the current scene state summary and that element must be of type 'text'.

effect.description: typewriter reveals characters one-at-a-time (good for testimonial phrases). wordByWord fades each word in sequence (good for measured arrivals). marquee slides the text horizontally (use sparingly — it draws the eye). shake trembles the text (for anxious or broken moments).

duration_ms.description: Animation span in milliseconds. 1500–4000 for a gradual reveal; 400–900 for a quick shake.

name: addP5Sketch
description: Place a localized p5.js sketch at a specific position. Used for gestural, short-lived visual responses — a lantern flickering, a hand drawing a line, a textile unfurling. Sketches MUST depict recognizable things. Up to 3 concurrent localized sketches; a 4th auto-retires the oldest (no rejection, you always get a sketch_id). Returns {sketch_id, retired_id?}.

position.description: One of the nine anchor positions.

size.description: Sketch canvas size. small ≈ 300×300, medium ≈ 500×500, large ≈ 800×800.

code.description: Complete p5.js sketch as JavaScript. See setP5Background for the window.features contract and the figurative-only constraint.

audio_reactive.description: If true, window.features updates every frame. If false, the sketch is self-contained.

lifetime_s.description: Optional lifetime in seconds. Omit for default permanence; supply a number for transient gestures that should retire automatically.

layer.description: Optional depth band for this localized sketch. Default is background (the sketch sits behind DOM elements). Pass midground or foreground only when the sketch should appear over images/text.
